analysis_summary: Telemetry rewrite A0 is now modeled as a builder-first execution contract: freeze the current snapshot/hydration baseline, define the bus-first event contract, establish the server projector, cut over reducer authority, demote snapshot transport, remove legacy writers, and validate runtime event to UI consumer traceability.

activities.title: Freeze Baseline Inventory
activities.description: Capture current snapshot, hydration, monitor, and page-hook telemetry behavior as baseline-only evidence before builder cutover.

activities.title: Define Event Contract
activities.description: Specify authoritative runtime telemetry event facts, ordering, replay, and downstream projector delivery rules.

activities.title: Establish Projector Authority
activities.description: Make the server-side telemetry projector the only authoritative read-model owner for session telemetry.

activities.title: Cut Over Reducer Authority
activities.description: Move canonical session_telemetry ownership to app global-sync reducer and force UI surfaces into pure-consumer mode.

activities.title: Demote Snapshot Transport
activities.description: Restrict session.top to bootstrap, catch-up, reconnect, buffer-miss, and degraded recovery transport only.

activities.title: Remove Legacy Telemetry Writers
activities.description: Delete or degrade hydration-first, monitor-first, page-hook-first, and fallback-based telemetry writers after authority cutover.

activities.title: Validate Canonical Telemetry Flow
activities.description: Prove steady-state propagation follows runtime event to projector to reducer to UI, with session.top limited to recovery paths.

arrows.label: Planning Docs And Existing Telemetry Evidence
Runtime Event Builder And Contract Spec
Bus Subscriber SSE And Projector Storage
Global Sync Reducer And UI Consumer Surfaces
Bootstrap Catch Up Degraded Only Policy
Validated Runtime To UI Consumer Contract

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Freeze current snapshot, hydration, monitor, and page-hook telemetry behavior as baseline-only inventory.
Condition: baseline paths are recorded and marked as non-target authority

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Define the runtime telemetry event contract, including fact shape, ordering, replay, and idempotency semantics.
Condition: authoritative bus-first event contract is approved for projector consumption

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Establish the server projector as sole telemetry read-model authority and route downstream state from projector ownership.
Condition: projector aggregate and downstream delivery contract are defined without duplicate authority

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Cut over canonical session telemetry ownership to the app reducer and require UI surfaces to stay pure consumers.
Condition: reducer canonical slice is defined and legacy steady-state writers are forbidden

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Demote session.top to bootstrap, reconnect, catch-up, buffer-miss, and degraded recovery transport only.
Condition: snapshot path is explicitly limited to recovery-only use

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Remove hydration-first, monitor-first, page-hook-first, and fallback-based telemetry writers that conflict with single authority.
Condition: conflicting legacy writers are removed or degraded with no authority relapse

grafcet_descriptor.StepAction: Validate that steady-state telemetry flows runtime event to projector to reducer to UI consumer, with session.top used only for recovery.
Condition: validation proves no duplicate authority fallback promotion or architecture drift remains

assumptions: Current monitor and hydration behavior is preserved only as baseline evidence, not as target architecture.
Runtime telemetry facts remain the upstream source for prompt, round, compaction, and future telemetry event families.
Server-side projector output may feed session.top for recovery without allowing session.top to regain steady-state authority.
App global-sync remains the canonical telemetry slice owner for all web telemetry surfaces.
validation_notes: A0 scope now mirrors tasks.md execution order exactly: baseline freeze, event contract, projector, reducer cutover, snapshot demotion, cleanup, validation.
session.top is modeled only as bootstrap/catch-up/degraded transport and never as steady-state authority.
UI authority is excluded from the model; UI appears only as reducer-consuming surface within the cutover and validation stages.
decision_trace: Current snapshot, hydration, monitor, and page-hook paths are treated as baseline inventory only.
Builder execution is organized around phased authority transfer instead of current-code convenience modules.
Server projector remains the only read-model authority, reducer remains the only app-state authority, and UI remains a pure consumer.
Snapshot transport is intentionally sequenced after reducer cutover so recovery cannot silently re-promote itself to steady-state authority.
boundary_map: Baseline boundary: current snapshot, hydration, monitor, and fallback paths recorded as inventory-only
Runtime boundary: telemetry fact emission and bus-first event contract
Server boundary: telemetry projector plus recovery-only snapshot transport
App boundary: global-sync canonical telemetry slice
evidence_trace: tasks.md defines the required execution sequence from baseline freeze through validation.
architecture.md defines current-state snapshot/hydration authority drift and target bus-first ownership.
Runtime events already exist in llm.ts and processor.ts.
session.top currently serves monitor snapshot transport.
use-status-monitor.ts currently refreshes session.top and sync.tsx hydrates session_telemetry.
confidence_notes: High confidence on current-state baseline evidence and target ownership rules because both are explicitly documented in architecture.md and tasks.md.
Medium confidence on final projector-to-reducer transport shape because the downstream event schema remains a planned contract rather than implemented code.
High confidence that the updated A0 artifacts now support builder execution traceability instead of describing current-code convenience paths.
